about.missionP1: The Cryptid Research Foundation (CRF) is a private research institution dedicated to collecting, analyzing, and systematically documenting sighting reports of unidentified creatures — commonly known as cryptids — from around the world.
about.missionP2: Since its founding in 1987, CRF has accumulated over 1,200 investigation reports through a network of field researchers. Our unique approach combines scientific methodology with local folklore and ethnological knowledge to uncover the truth behind unknown creatures.
about.missionP3: This database aims to make CRF's investigation records publicly available, fostering interest and understanding in cryptid research.
about.teamCaption: CRF Field Research Team — Annual Expedition, 2024
about.classDescription: CRF applies a proprietary classification system based on each cryptid's threat level. Used for ensuring investigator safety and selecting appropriate response protocols.
about.statusDescription: Each cryptid investigation file is assigned one of the following statuses.
about.siteP1: This site is a database of unidentified mysterious animals (UMA / Cryptids). Explore folklore, sighting reports, and scientific analysis of cryptids from around the world.
about.siteP2: We also create original merchandise featuring cryptid designs.

CLASS-I.name: Harmless
CLASS-I.description: No confirmed threat to humans. Observation and documentation only.

CLASS-II.name: Caution
CLASS-II.description: Few reports of direct harm, but caution is advised upon encounter. Unwarranted approach not recommended.

CLASS-III.name: Dangerous
CLASS-III.description: Multiple reports of attacks on humans. Teams of multiple investigators required with protective equipment.

CLASS-IV.name: High Threat
CLASS-IV.description: Fatalities or major damage reported. Only specially trained investigators may respond. General researchers prohibited.

CLASS-V.name: Critical
CLASS-V.description: Maximum threat level. Survival rate drops significantly upon encounter. Remote investigation only. HQ special authorization required.

CLASS-S.name: Catastrophic
CLASS-S.description: Special class. Threat equivalent to a regional disaster. All records classified top secret. Response protocols under direct board oversight. All disclosure prohibited.
